title: Optimise UE
description: A comprehensive diagnostic and optimization framework for Unreal Engine. Audit assets, analyze material complexity, inspect draw calls, and manage level-wide performance from a centralized command center.

sections.title: Overview
body: Optimise UE is a comprehensive diagnostic and optimization framework designed to empower Technical Directors and Artists to identify, quantify, and resolve performance bottlenecks in complex virtual production environments.

The suite provides a centralized command center to audit assets, analyze material complexity, inspect draw calls, and manage level-wide optimizations like light configurations and cull distances - all without leaving the editor.

sections.title: Asset Auditor
body: Scans the entire project for common asset problems: oversized textures, high-polygon meshes, assets with missing or inadequate LODs, and unused assets consuming disk and memory.
Outputs a prioritised report with actionable fix suggestions so artists and TDs can quickly identify what is hurting performance before a shoot or render.

sections.title: Nanite Audit
body: Evaluates static meshes to determine whether enabling Nanite makes practical sense based on geometry density, material slots, and LOD setup. Scores each mesh and provides a prioritized recommendation list.

sections.title: LOD Auto-Generator
body: Batch-generates Level of Detail (LOD) meshes for static meshes using Unreal's built-in reduction tools. Includes preview before applying and full Session History rollback - changes are non-destructive.

sections.title: Material Complexity Analyser
body: Scans materials and material instances under a chosen folder and scores each one across five cost dimensions: Blend mode (flags Translucent, Additive, Modulate), Shading model (flags Subsurface, Hair, Cloth, Eye), Texture count, World Position Offset, and Pixel Depth Offset.

sections.title: Draw Call Inspector
body: Scans static meshes to identify small props (default < 500 triangles) that do not justify individual draw calls. Flags merge candidates in Red (Merge) or Yellow (Review) and summarizes potential savings by shared materials. Exports to CSV.

sections.title: Light Optimiser
body: Scans placed actors in the active level to flag expensive lighting configurations: Movable shadow casters, small-radius shadow maps on fill lights (default < 300cm radius), and overlapping light influence volumes.

sections.title: Stats Dashboard
body: A live performance panel updating every 250ms. Displays FPS (color-coded for film targets), CPU/GPU frame times, RAM usage, and real-time tracking of pending shader compile jobs.

sections.title: Texture Optimizer
body: Batch-resizes textures via MaxTextureSize - a GPU-side cap that is fully non-destructive. Supports Proportional (fraction-based, e.g. halving 8K to 4K) and Absolute (fixed power-of-two cap, e.g. all to 2048px) modes.

sections.title: Level Warmup
body: A structured pass to eliminate on-set technical hitches. Includes: Shader Pre-compilation (blocks the editor until all queued shaders are finished) and Mip Residency (forces textures to full resolution to prevent blurry-to-sharp popping during a shoot).

sections.body: Always back up your project before running batch operations like Texture Optimizer or LOD Auto-Generator. Session History allows rollback but a project backup is the safest net.
